headline: Unilateral Child Conversion Remains Unresolved After Indira Gandhi Ruling
context: Despite the Federal Court's 2018 Indira Gandhi ruling requiring both parents' consent for child conversion, at least 14 unilateral conversions have been registered since the decision.

cards.big: The Federal Court ruled in 2018: both parents must consent to a child's conversion.
cards.sub: 14 unilateral conversions were registered after the ruling. State religious authorities processed them regardless.

cards.big: 8 states have not amended their Islamic family law to comply with the ruling.
cards.sub: State enactments still allow one parent to convert a child. Federal court rulings do not automatically amend state law.

cards.big: Indira Gandhi has not seen her youngest child since 2009.
cards.sub: 16 years after the conversion dispute began, custody enforcement remains incomplete.

cards.big: The Law Reform (Marriage and Divorce) Amendment Bill was tabled in 2017.
cards.sub: It was withdrawn after objections. No replacement bill has been tabled in 9 years.

cards.big: A Federal Court ruling that 8 states ignore is not a ruling. It is a suggestion to a system that does not listen.
Constitutional supremacy means Federal Court rulings bind all authorities. If state non-compliance continues, the federal system itself is at stake — not just conversion law.
